revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} '''usurped''' is for displaying square and bare ''archive'' URLs, when the underlying source URL has been [[Wikipedia:Link rot/Usurpations|usurped]]. It is not the same as template {{tlx|unfit}}, see below when to use "unfit" vs "usurped". *Square URL: {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} *Bare URL: {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} The template wraps it so future processes are aware it is a usurped domain. This prevents tools like IABot, reFill and Citation bot (and humans) from converting square or bare archive URLs into a CS1 or CS2 template and thus accidentally re-activating the usurped URL. It is important to use 1= otherwise any "=" in the URL itself will cause a problem. == Unfit vs. Usurped == When to use templates {{tld|usurped}} or {{tlx|unfit}}? * '''usurped''' is a URL at a website that has been hijacked, such as a gambling site or domain reseller. The entire website is a problem because it's faking its true identity. * '''unfit''' is a URL at a website that is real (not fake), but this particular URL is a problem for Wikipedia. Includes spam, malware - making it unfit for consumption, like a rotten egg hiding among good eggs. Not be confused with an inoperable link - in that case there is {{para|archive-url}} + {{para|url-status|dead}}, or if no archive exists {{tlx|dead link}}. In the vast majority of cases "dead" or "usurped" are the correct options. There are some edge cases where "unfit" is appropriate. If you are unsure, use "dead" or "usurped". == How to use == If a usurped URL is bare or square, and is also an archive, wrap it in {{tld|usurped}} as follows: * {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} * {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} If a usurped URL has no archive available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. If the usurped URL is in a CS1 or CS2 template such as {{tlx|cite web}} simply change {{para|url-status|usurped}} and add an archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} If a CS1 or CS2 template and no archive is available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. Decision tree of the above steps: [[WP:USURPURL]] == Conversion to CS1 or CS2 == It is recommended to convert citations using {{tld|usurped}} to CS1 or CS2, e.g. {{tlx|cite web}}, where it makes sense. Add {{para|url-status|usurped}} and the archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] }} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Reference", "description": "The reference that will have a usurped tag.", "required": true, "example": "[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]", "type": "string" } }, "description": "This template will add a tag to a source, notifying readers that this source has been usurped.", "format": "inline" }

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} '''usurped''' is for displaying square and bare ''archive'' URLs, when the underlying source URL has been [[Wikipedia:Link rot/Usurpations|usurped]]. It is not the same as template {{tlx|unfit}}, see below when to use "unfit" vs "usurped". *Square URL: {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} *Bare URL: {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} The template wraps it so future processes are aware it is a usurped domain. This prevents tools like IABot, reFill and Citation bot (and humans) from converting square or bare archive URLs into a CS1 or CS2 template and thus accidentally re-activating the usurped URL. It is important to use 1= otherwise any "=" in the URL itself will cause a problem. == Unfit vs. Usurped == When to use templates {{tld|usurped}} or {{tlx|unfit}}? * '''usurped''' is a URL at a website that has been hijacked, such as a gambling site or domain reseller. The entire website is a problem because it's faking its true identity. * '''unfit''' is a URL at a website that is real (not fake), but this particular URL is a problem for Wikipedia. Includes spam, malware - making it unfit for consumption, like a rotten egg hiding among good eggs. Not be confused with an inoperable link - in that case there is {{para|archive-url}} + {{para|url-status|dead}}, or if no archive exists {{tlx|dead link}}. In the vast majority of cases "dead" or "usurped" are the correct options. There are some edge cases where "unfit" is appropriate. If you are unsure, use "dead" or "usurped". == How to use == If a usurped URL is bare or square, and is also an archive, wrap it in {{tld|usurped}} as follows: * {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} * {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} If a usurped URL has no archive available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. If the usurped URL is in a CS1 or CS2 template such as {{tlx|cite web}} simply change {{para|url-status|usurped}} and add an archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} If a CS1 or CS2 template and no archive is available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. Decision tree of the above steps: [[WP:USURPURL]] == Conversion to CS1 or CS2 == It is recommended to convert citations using {{tld|usurped}} to CS1 or CS2, e.g. {{tlx|cite web}}, where it makes sense. Add {{para|url-status|usurped}} and the archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] }} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Reference", "description": "The reference that will have a usurped tag.", "required": true, "example": "[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]", "type": "string" } }, "description": "This template will add a tag to a source, notifying readers that this source has been usurped.", "format": "inline" } [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} '''usurped''' is for displaying square and bare ''archive'' URLs, when the underlying source URL has been [[Wikipedia:Link rot/Usurpations|usurped]]. It is not the same as template {{tlx|unfit}}, see below when to use "unfit" vs "usurped". *Square URL: {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} *Bare URL: {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} The template wraps it so future processes are aware it is a usurped domain. This prevents tools like IABot, reFill and Citation bot (and humans) from converting square or bare archive URLs into a CS1 or CS2 template and thus accidentally re-activating the usurped URL. It is important to use 1= otherwise any "=" in the URL itself will cause a problem. == Unfit vs. Usurped == When to use templates {{tld|usurped}} or {{tlx|unfit}}? * '''usurped''' is a URL at a website that has been hijacked, such as a gambling site or domain reseller. The entire website is a problem because it's faking its true identity. * '''unfit''' is a URL at a website that is real (not fake), but this particular URL is a problem for Wikipedia. Includes spam, malware - making it unfit for consumption, like a rotten egg hiding among good eggs. Not be confused with an inoperable link - in that case there is {{para|archive-url}} + {{para|url-status|dead}}, or if no archive exists {{tlx|dead link}}. In the vast majority of cases "dead" or "usurped" are the correct options. There are some edge cases where "unfit" is appropriate. If you are unsure, use "dead" or "usurped". == How to use == If a usurped URL is bare or square, and is also an archive, wrap it in {{tld|usurped}} as follows: * {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} * {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} If a usurped URL has no archive available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. If the usurped URL is in a CS1 or CS2 template such as {{tlx|cite web}} simply change {{para|url-status|usurped}} and add an archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} If a CS1 or CS2 template and no archive is available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. Decision tree of the above steps: [[WP:USURPURL]] == Conversion to CS1 or CS2 == It is recommended to convert citations using {{tld|usurped}} to CS1 or CS2, e.g. {{tlx|cite web}}, where it makes sense. Add {{para|url-status|usurped}} and the archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] }} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Reference", "description": "The reference that will have a usurped tag.", "required": true, "example": "[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]", "type": "string" } }, "description": "This template will add a tag to a source, notifying readers that this source has been usurped.", "format": "inline" }

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} '''usurped''' is for displaying square and bare ''archive'' URLs, when the underlying source URL has been [[Wikipedia:Link rot/Usurpations|usurped]]. It is not the same as template {{tlx|unfit}}, see below when to use "unfit" vs "usurped". *Square URL: {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} *Bare URL: {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} The template wraps it so future processes are aware it is a usurped domain. This prevents tools like IABot, reFill and Citation bot (and humans) from converting square or bare archive URLs into a CS1 or CS2 template and thus accidentally re-activating the usurped URL. It is important to use 1= otherwise any "=" in the URL itself will cause a problem. == Unfit vs. Usurped == When to use templates {{tld|usurped}} or {{tlx|unfit}}? * '''usurped''' is a URL at a website that has been hijacked, such as a gambling site or domain reseller. The entire website is a problem because it's faking its true identity. * '''unfit''' is a URL at a website that is real (not fake), but this particular URL is a problem for Wikipedia. Includes spam, malware - making it unfit for consumption, like a rotten egg hiding among good eggs. Not be confused with an inoperable link - in that case there is {{para|archive-url}} + {{para|url-status|dead}}, or if no archive exists {{tlx|dead link}}. In the vast majority of cases "dead" or "usurped" are the correct options. There are some edge cases where "unfit" is appropriate. If you are unsure, use "dead" or "usurped". == How to use == If a usurped URL is bare or square, and is also an archive, wrap it in {{tld|usurped}} as follows: * {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} * {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} If a usurped URL has no archive available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. If the usurped URL is in a CS1 or CS2 template such as {{tlx|cite web}} simply change {{para|url-status|usurped}} and add an archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} If a CS1 or CS2 template and no archive is available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. Decision tree of the above steps: [[WP:USURPURL]] == Conversion to CS1 or CS2 == It is recommended to convert citations using {{tld|usurped}} to CS1 or CS2, e.g. {{tlx|cite web}}, where it makes sense. Add {{para|url-status|usurped}} and the archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} {{sandbox other|| [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] }} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Reference", "description": "The reference that will have a usurped tag.", "required": true, "example": "[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]", "type": "string" } }, "description": "This template will add a tag to a source, notifying readers that this source has been usurped.", "format": "inline" } [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23692667944|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} '''usurped''' is for displaying square and bare ''archive'' URLs, when the underlying source URL has been [[Wikipedia:Link rot/Usurpations|usurped]]. It is not the same as template {{tlx|unfit}}, see below when to use "unfit" vs "usurped". *Square URL: {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} *Bare URL: {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} The template wraps it so future processes are aware it is a usurped domain. This prevents tools like IABot, reFill and Citation bot (and humans) from converting square or bare archive URLs into a CS1 or CS2 template and thus accidentally re-activating the usurped URL. It is important to use 1= otherwise any "=" in the URL itself will cause a problem. == Unfit vs. Usurped == When to use templates {{tld|usurped}} or {{tlx|unfit}}? * '''usurped''' is a URL at a website that has been hijacked, such as a gambling site or domain reseller. The entire website is a problem because it's faking its true identity. * '''unfit''' is a URL at a website that is real (not fake), but this particular URL is a problem for Wikipedia. Includes spam, malware - making it unfit for consumption, like a rotten egg hiding among good eggs. Not be confused with an inoperable link - in that case there is {{para|archive-url}} + {{para|url-status|dead}}, or if no archive exists {{tlx|dead link}}. In the vast majority of cases "dead" or "usurped" are the correct options. There are some edge cases where "unfit" is appropriate. If you are unsure, use "dead" or "usurped". == How to use == If a usurped URL is bare or square, and is also an archive, wrap it in {{tld|usurped}} as follows: * {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} * {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} If a usurped URL has no archive available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. If the usurped URL is in a CS1 or CS2 template such as {{tlx|cite web}} simply change {{para|url-status|usurped}} and add an archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} If a CS1 or CS2 template and no archive is available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. Decision tree of the above steps: [[WP:USURPURL]] == Conversion to CS1 or CS2 == It is recommended to convert citations using {{tld|usurped}} to CS1 or CS2, e.g. {{tlx|cite web}}, where it makes sense. Add {{para|url-status|usurped}} and the archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} {{sandbox other|| }} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Reference", "description": "The reference that will have a usurped tag.", "required": true, "example": "[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]", "type": "string" } }, "description": "This template will add a tag to a source, notifying readers that this source has been usurped.", "format": "inline" } [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24999656626|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} '''usurped''' is for displaying square and bare ''archive'' URLs, when the underlying source URL has been [[Wikipedia:Link rot/Usurpations|usurped]]. It is not the same as template {{tlx|unfit}}, see below when to use "unfit" vs "usurped". *Square URL: {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} *Bare URL: {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} The template wraps it so future processes are aware it is a usurped domain. This prevents tools like IABot, reFill and Citation bot (and humans) from converting square or bare archive URLs into a CS1 or CS2 template and thus accidentally re-activating the usurped URL. It is important to use 1= otherwise any "=" in the URL itself will cause a problem. == Unfit vs. Usurped == When to use templates {{tld|usurped}} or {{tlx|unfit}}? * '''usurped''' is a URL at a website that has been hijacked, such as a gambling site or domain reseller. The entire website is a problem because it's faking its true identity. * '''unfit''' is a URL at a website that is real (not fake), but this particular URL is a problem for Wikipedia. Includes spam, malware - making it unfit for consumption, like a rotten egg hiding among good eggs. Not be confused with an inoperable link - in that case there is {{para|archive-url}} + {{para|url-status|dead}}, or if no archive exists {{tlx|dead link}}. In the vast majority of cases "dead" or "usurped" are the correct options. There are some edge cases where "unfit" is appropriate. If you are unsure, use "dead" or "usurped". == How to use == If a usurped URL is bare or square, and is also an archive, wrap it in {{tld|usurped}} as follows: * {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]}} * {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} :''produces'': {{usurped|1=https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com}} If a usurped URL has no archive available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. If the usurped URL is in a CS1 or CS2 template such as {{tlx|cite web}} simply change {{para|url-status|usurped}} and add an archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} If a CS1 or CS2 template and no archive is available, the URL or citation should be deleted entirely from Wikipedia as non-verifiable per [[WP:V]]. If the citation would be verifiable without a URL simply delete the URL; otherwise, delete the entire citation. Decision tree of the above steps: [[WP:USURPURL]] == Conversion to CS1 or CS2 == It is recommended to convert citations using {{tld|usurped}} to CS1 or CS2, e.g. {{tlx|cite web}}, where it makes sense. Add {{para|url-status|usurped}} and the archive URL to {{para|archive-url}} {{sandbox other|| }} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Reference", "description": "The reference that will have a usurped tag.", "required": true, "example": "[https://web.archive.org/web/2011010101/http://usurped-domain.com Example]", "type": "string" } }, "description": "This template will add a tag to a source, notifying readers that this source has been usurped.", "format": "inline" } [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
